subject: Exploring Iceberg migration from Snowflake
body:
Hi,

I'm Jordan Chen, Head of Data Engineering at Acme Corp. We're a Series D fintech company (~200 engineers, 40 on data). We currently run Snowflake Enterprise on AWS with about 80TB of data, ingesting ~500GB/day from Salesforce, PostgreSQL (main transactional DB), and Kafka streams from our payment processing service.

Our stack:
- Snowflake Enterprise (compute + storage)
- dbt-core 1.8 for transformations (~400 models, longest run is 4.5 hours)
- Airflow 2.9 on MWAA for orchestration
- Some Spark 3.5 jobs on EMR for ML feature engineering
- S3 for raw landing zone

Pain points:
1. Snowflake costs have grown 3x YoY and we're hitting our contract ceiling
2. dbt runs are getting slower — our nightly batch takes 4.5 hours and we need it under 2
3. We want to query the same data from both Spark (ML team) and our BI tools without maintaining copies
4. Vendor lock-in concerns — we want to own our data format

We've been reading about Iceberg and Tower looks interesting. Can we set up a call to discuss whether Tower could help us migrate off Snowflake while keeping our dbt + Airflow workflows intact?

Also cc'ing our VP of Engineering, Sarah Kim (sarah.kim@acmecorp.com), who owns the budget for this initiative.

Best,
Jordan
